title: carduncellus monspelliensium subsp. matritensis
intro: Carduncellus monspelliensium subsp. matritensis, also known as the Madrid cardoon, is a subspecies of Carduncellus monspelliensium, a plant in the thistle family native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be used in culinary dishes, adding to its versatility as a medicinal and edible plant.
